message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-8, latest commit). Your job: confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; the regression test actually DISCRIMINATES — fails if the fix is reverted). Be skeptical; try to find a hole.

FINDING K-OBS-2:
pipeline.py _log_trail: a hashable-but-non-JSON payload value (bytes/datetime — freeze() only rejects UNhashable) caused json.dump to raise TypeError INSIDE the NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) block BEFORE `path = fh.name`, orphaning the temp file (cleanup unreachable) AND silently dropping the #G-009 artifact (track() swallowed it, no warning). Fix: serialize up front with json.dumps(..., default=str) (so it can't raise mid-write and the artifact still attaches), open the temp file with fh.name always assigned so the finally cleanup is reachable, and add a _log.warning in track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent. Test: test_provenance_artifact_survives_non_json_evidence. VERIFY no temp-file leak AND the artifact attaches with such a value.

Read the actual changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path> — use "python -m pytest", not bare pytest, for your own runs; uv 0.11.21 is available for K-OPS-1). Judge ONLY K-OBS-2. Return CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name it); REJECTED if wrong or non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding K-OBS-2. Let me start by examining the changed files and the regression test.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -8 && echo "---" && git show --stat HEAD

---
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

content.text: Let me look at the actual diff for pipeline.py and the regression test.

content.content:
commit d08f03147df786fce18af280d398d9b51ff71feb
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 11:22:01 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p8): 8 confirmed findings incl. 3 CI-red blockers — main was failing
    
    Main CI had been RED (bypassed by --admin merges) on THREE counts, plus assessment
    found more. All 2-agent verified (or dispositively confirmed by the red CI logs):
    
    CI-RED (critical path to green):
    - K-CI-2 (high): CI ran the bare `pytest` console script, which (unlike `python -m
      pytest`) does not put the repo root on sys.path → `from tests import ...` failed at
      collection (`No module named 'tests'`); the suite NEVER ran in CI. Fix: pythonpath
      += "." so `tests` imports under any invocation. Now collects 555 tests.
    - K-CI-1 (high): core (no-extras) lane mypy failed on seam.py's guarded `import httpx`
      (httpx ships with the reasoning extra). Fix: httpx.* in mypy ignore_missing_imports.
    - K-OPS-1 (high): lockfile "reproducibility" gate re-resolved against live PyPI (no
      --exclude-newer), going red whenever any in-range transitive published — it was
      failing on clean checkouts. Fix: pin resolution via --exclude-newer sourced from a
      single committed date file (.uv-exclude-newer), in CI + regenerate_lockfiles.sh.
      Chose a cutoff (2026-06-14) clear of a release straddling the boundary (arize-phoenix
      17.6/17.7) where uv resolution was non-deterministic; regenerated all three locks.
    
    CODE (assessment survivors, 2-agent verified true-positive):
    - K-REL-1 (high): SIGTERM trace-flush handler swallowed termination when prior was
      SIG_DFL (the default) → process unkillable by docker stop/kill until SIGKILL. Fix:
      re-establish SIG_DFL and re-deliver the signal so default-terminate still runs.
    - K-OBS-2 (high): provenance artifact temp file leaked + artifact silently dropped when
      a payload held a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes/datetime). Fix: serialize up front
      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py b/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
index 2f09609..1e40525 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
@@ -322,6 +322,14 @@ def run_study(
             fn(tracker, run_id)
         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
             trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
+            # Emit a structured warning too (#K-OBS-2): a tracker call failing used to be recorded
+            # ONLY to the trail, so e.g. a provenance-artifact serialization failure dropped the
+            # #G-009 artifact silently — an operator auditing the run saw no artifact and no reason.
+            _log.warning(
+                "experiment tracker call failed (%s: %s); this metric/artifact was not logged",
+                type(exc).__name__,
+                exc,
+            )
 
     def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
         # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
@@ -737,19 +745,31 @@ def run_study(
 
                 from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
 
-                with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
+                # Serialize FIRST, with a JSON-safe fallback (#K-OBS-2). freeze() accepts any
+                # HASHABLE leaf, so a payload may carry a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes,
+                # datetime, Enum) — e.g. in gate evidence. The old code called json.dump INSIDE the
+                # NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) block BEFORE `path = fh.name`, so a TypeError
+                # there (a) orphaned the temp file (cleanup unreachable — path never assigned) AND
+                # (b) was swallowed by track() so the #G-009 artifact silently never attached.
+                # Building the string up front with default=str means serialization can't leak a
+                # file and the artifact still attaches even with such values.
+                payload = json.dumps(
+                    list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), indent=2, default=str
+                )
+                fh = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
                     "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
-                ) as fh:
-                    json.dump(list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), fh, indent=2)
-                    path = fh.name
+                )
                 # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
                 # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
-                # (#H-013). The tracker reads/copies synchronously in log_artifact.
+                # (#H-013). `fh.name` is always assigned (the file exists once created), so the
+                # finally cleanup is always reachable. The tracker copies synchronously.
                 try:
-                    t.log_artifact(rid, path)
+                    fh.write(payload)
+                    fh.close()
+                    t.log_artifact(rid, fh.name)
                 finally:
                     try:
-                        os.unlink(path)
+                        os.unlink(fh.name)
                     except OSError:
                         pass

      with default=str (always assigns path → cleanup reachable; artifact attaches); warn in
      track() so a tracker failure is no longer silent.
    - K-SEC-1 (med): local-URI guard rejected Unix-domain-socket Postgres hosts (leading
      '/'), the most data-sovereign connection. Fix: accept a single leading-'/' socket path,
      keep UNC (//, /\) rejection.
    
    OPS:
    - K-OPS-2 (med): regenerate_lockfiles.sh omitted requirements-build.lock (which CI gates).
      Fix: regenerate all three; point CI errors at the script.
    - K-OPS-3 (med): coverage measured but never enforced. Fix: --cov-fail-under=90 (current ~96.6%).
    
    Regression tests added for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean;
    555 passed, 5 skipped; coverage 96.58% (floor 90). Lockfiles reproducible (script == CI regen).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/tests/test_pipeline.py b/tests/test_pipeline.py
index 48b86d2..2ed0875 100644
--- a/tests/test_pipeline.py
+++ b/tests/test_pipeline.py
@@ -467,6 +467,50 @@ def test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort(tmp_path):
     assert any(e.kind == "tracking_error" for e in res2.provenance)
 
 
+def test_provenance_artifact_survives_non_json_evidence(tmp_path):
+    # #K-OBS-2: freeze() accepts any HASHABLE leaf, so a payload may carry a hashable-but-non-JSON
+    # value (bytes/datetime). Pre-fix, _log_trail called json.dump INSIDE the temp-file block before
+    # assigning the path, so the TypeError (a) orphaned the temp file (leak) and (b) was swallowed
+    # by track(), silently dropping the #G-009 artifact. Now serialized up front with default=str:
+    # the artifact still attaches AND no temp file leaks.
+    import datetime as _dt
+    import os as _os
+
+    trail = ProvenanceTrail()
+    trail.record(
+        "gate",
+        "evidence with non-json leaves",
+        evidence={"token": b"\x00rawbytes-secretish", "when": _dt.datetime(2026, 1, 1)},
+    )
+
+    class CapTracker:
+        def __init__(self):
+            self.artifacts = []
+            self.contents = ""
+
+        def start_run(self, name, params):
+            return "run-1"
+
+        def log_metric(self, *a):
+            pass
+
+        def log_artifact(self, run_id, path):
+            self.artifacts.append(path)
+            assert _os.path.exists(path), "tracker cannot read the artifact (temp file gone early)"
+            with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
+                self.contents = fh.read()
+
+        def end_run(self, *a, **k):
+            pass
+
+    t = CapTracker()
+    _run(tmp_path, ALL, tracker=t, trail=trail)
+    prov = [p for p in t.artifacts if str(p).endswith("_provenance.json")]
+    assert prov, "provenance artifact was silently dropped on non-JSON evidence (#K-OBS-2)"
+    assert not any(_os.path.exists(p) for p in prov), "temp file leaked (#K-OBS-2/#H-013)"
+    assert "rawbytes-secretish" in t.contents, "non-JSON value lost instead of serialized via str"
+
+
 def test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error(tmp_path):
     # #F-008: a storage failure in the optional versioner seam must surface as the documented
     # IngestionError, not a raw untyped exception the caller cannot distinguish from a firewall

713	        if verify_trail:
714	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
715	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
716	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
717	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
718	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
719	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
720	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
721	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
722	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
723	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
724	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
725	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
726	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
727	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
728	        trail.record(
729	            "run_end",
730	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
731	            study=defn.name,
732	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
733	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
734	        )
735	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
736	        # Correlate the hash-chained trail to the experiment run (#G-009): persist it as a JSON
737	        # artifact on the tracker so an auditor opening the run can retrieve the provenance that
738	        # produced its metrics. Best-effort, like the other tracker calls — never aborts the run.
739	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
740	
741	            def _log_trail(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
742	                import json
743	                import os
744	                import tempfile
745	
746	                from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
747	
748	                # Serialize FIRST, with a JSON-safe fallback (#K-OBS-2). freeze() accepts any
749	                # HASHABLE leaf, so a payload may carry a hashable-but-non-JSON value (bytes,
750	                # datetime, Enum) — e.g. in gate evidence. The old code called json.dump INSIDE the
751	                # NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) block BEFORE `path = fh.name`, so a TypeError
752	                # there (a) orphaned the temp file (cleanup unreachable — path never assigned) AND
753	                # (b) was swallowed by track() so the #G-009 artifact silently never attached.
754	                # Building the string up front with default=str means serialization can't leak a
755	                # file and the artifact still attaches even with such values.
756	                payload = json.dumps(
757	                    list(entries_to_records(list(trail.entries))), indent=2, default=str
758	                )
759	                fh = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
760	                    "w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, encoding="utf-8"
761	                )
762	                # Clean up the scratch file after the tracker has copied it into its store, so a
763	                # tracked run does not leak a temp file (with provenance contents) on every call
764	                # (#H-013). `fh.name` is always assigned (the file exists once created), so the
765	                # finally cleanup is always reachable. The tracker copies synchronously.
766	                try:
767	                    fh.write(payload)
768	                    fh.close()
769	                    t.log_artifact(rid, fh.name)
770	                finally:
771	                    try:
772	                        os.unlink(fh.name)
773	                    except OSError:
774	                        pass
775	
776	            track(_log_trail)
777	        ok = True
778	    except Exception as exc:
779	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
780	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
781	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
782	        try:
783	            trail.record(
784	                "run_failed",
785	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
786	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
787	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
788	                last_phase=last_phase,
789	            )

226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """

236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238

`persist_trail` at line 241 is a separate public API (caller-invoked, writes to a caller-given path, returns it) — not part of the silent best-effort tracker path, and out of scope for K-OBS-2 (it raises visibly to the caller, no temp-file leak, no silent drop). The finding is specifically about `_log_trail`. I'll note it but it's not a residual gap in this fix.

content.content:

plain json.dumps raises TypeError (root cause confirmed): Object of type bytes is not JSON serializable

content.text:
All confirmed. The root-cause premise holds: `freeze()` admits bytes/datetime, plain `json.dumps` raises `TypeError`, and `default=str` serializes it. The fix restores cleanly, test passes on fixed and fails on reverted (discriminates), failing for the correct reason (silent artifact drop).

Summary of judgment:

**Appropriate** — Correct root cause and layer. The fix targets `_log_trail` (the silent best-effort tracker path, #G-009). It addresses all three sub-issues precisely:
1. Serializes up front via `json.dumps(..., default=str)` so the `TypeError` cannot occur mid-write inside the temp-file block.
2. `fh.name` is always assigned once the file is created, making the `finally`/`os.unlink` cleanup reachable (no orphaned temp file).
3. Adds `_log.warning` in `track()` so any tracker failure (not just this one) is no longer silent.

**Complete / discriminating** — Reverting only the `_log_trail` body to the pre-fix form makes the test fail with `assert prov` (artifact silently dropped, empty list); the fixed form passes. The test independently checks all three guarantees: artifact attaches (`prov` non-empty), temp file is readable during `log_artifact` but gone after (no leak), and the bytes value is preserved as a string in the content. The `default=str` premise and the `freeze`-admits-bytes premise both verified directly.

Minor non-blocking observation (out of scope for K-OBS-2): `ResultBundle.persist_trail` at pipeline.py:241 uses `json.dumps(... entries_to_records ...)` WITHOUT `default=str`, so it would still raise on a bytes/datetime leaf. But that is a caller-invoked public API that raises visibly to the caller (no temp-file leak, no silent drop), so it is a different code path, not a residual gap in the `_log_trail` fix.

input.rationale: Fix is appropriate and complete. Root cause verified directly: freeze() admits hashable-but-non-JSON leaves (bytes/datetime), and plain json.dumps over entries_to_records raises TypeError on them, while json.dumps(..., default=str) succeeds. The fix in pipeline.py _log_trail (lines 756-774) does exactly the right three things at the right layer: (1) serializes the payload up front with default=str so the TypeError can no longer fire mid-write inside the NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) block; (2) restructures so fh.name is always assigned the moment the file exists, making the finally/os.unlink cleanup reachable (no orphaned temp file); (3) adds _log.warning in track() (lines 328-332) so a swallowed tracker failure is no longer silent. The regression test DISCRIMINATES: I reverted only the _log_trail body to the pre-fix with-block form and the test failed with 'provenance artifact was silently dropped on non-JSON evidence (#K-OBS-2)' (assert prov == []), and it passes on the restored fixed code (1 passed). The test independently exercises all three guarantees — artifact attaches (prov non-empty), temp file readable during log_artifact but absent afterward (no leak), and the bytes value preserved as a string in the artifact content. File restored to HEAD (git diff clean).
concerns: Non-blocking, out of scope for K-OBS-2: ResultBundle.persist_trail (pipeline.py:241) still calls json.dumps(entries_to_records(...)) WITHOUT default=str, so it would raise TypeError on the same bytes/datetime leaf. This is a separate caller-invoked public API that raises visibly to the caller with no temp-file leak and no silent drop, so it is a different code path rather than a residual gap in the _log_trail fix — but it is the same latent serialization fragility and could merit its own finding.

content.text: Judgment complete: **CONFIRMED**. The K-OBS-2 fix is both appropriate (correct root cause, sound up-front-serialization approach, right layer in `_log_trail`/`track`) and complete (regression test discriminates — fails on revert with the exact silent-drop symptom, passes on fix; no temp-file leak, artifact attaches with bytes/datetime). One non-blocking out-of-scope observation noted: the unrelated `persist_trail` public API has the same latent `json.dumps` fragility but is a visibly-raising caller path, not a residual gap in this fix.
